items.title: 'Wrong in law': Human rights lawyer says footballers' fast-tracked citizenship is void from outset
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The grant of citizenship to seven naturalised footballers under Article 19 of the Federal Constitution by Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail is unconstitutional and unlawful, and cannot be dismissed as a mere procedural irregularity, a lawyer said. Human rights lawyer Zaid Malek took issue with the Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission's (EAIC) finding that the home minister had the constitutional authority to grant citizenship to the seven players under Article 19(2) of the Federal Constitution, arguing that it overlooked the residency requirement under the same provision. The EAIC yesterday found procedural irregularities in the citizenship applications of the seven naturalised footballers but maintained that the Federal Constitution empowers the home minister to consider such applications under Article 19, read together with Section 20(1)(e) of Part III of the Second Schedule to the Federal Constitution.

items.title: 19 arrested as 'Geng Karim' ring dismantled
items.summary: © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Yong Peng PH candidate flags alleged campaigning after deadline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Yong Peng state constituency, Yong Hui Yi, has lodged an official complaint with the Election Commission (EC), alleging that Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Ling Tian Soon continued campaigning after the campaign period ended. Yong claimed that MCA Youth and Ling had continued campaigning on social media after the official campaign period ended, in breach of election regulations. Yong said the actions of MCA Youth and Ling after the campaign period had ended amounted to a violation of election regulations and the EC's reminder.

items.title: [LIVE] Johor election results - BN takes the lead in Skudai
items.summary: 5.16pm - Johor Jaya - PH's Lee Wern Yiing is leading with 98 seats, Astro Awani reports. 5.17pm - Jementah - PH's Ng Kor Sim is leading with 67 votes 5.24pm - Pekan nanas- PH'S Yeo Tung Siong is leading with 89 votes 5.34pm - Skudai - BN's Tan Hiang Kee is leading with 98 votes, Astro Awani reports 5.38pm - Voter turnout stood at 67.44 per cent as of 5pm. 5.38pm - Bukit Permai - BN's Mohd Jafni Md Shukor is leading with 87 votes, Astro Awani reports.

items.title: Johor polls: Perlis student braves 12-hour journey to cast vote
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 12-hour journey spanning hundreds of kilometres was no obstacle for first-time voter Aina Nazirah Rahmat, who travelled from Perlis to Johor to cast her ballot in the state election today. The 21-year-old Diploma in Information Technology student at Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Polytechnic in Arau was determined to vote despite having to alter her travel plans when express bus tickets from Arau to Batu Pahat sold out. "I was determined to return to Johor and vote even though the direct bus tickets to Batu Pahat were sold out.

items.title: Posthumous Penang state award honours Nordin Ahmad's enduring legacy
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The posthumous conferment of the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (DSPN) on the late Datuk Nordin Ahmad, 55 years after his passing, honours the enduring legacy of the Penang-born legendary actor and recognises his significant contributions to Malaysia's film industry. The late actor's nephew, Mohd Sabri Mohd Sidik, 60, said the award reflected the lasting impact of Nordin's contributions beyond acting, recognising his role as a director and scriptwriter who contributed significantly to the development of Malay cinema and elevated Penang's standing in the nation's arts scene. "I would like to convey my sincere appreciation and gratitude to the Penang state government for conferring the DSPN posthumously on the late Nordin Ahmad.

items.title: Johor polls: Onn Abu Bakar claims phantom voting at Senggarang polling centre
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan Senggarang state seat candidate Onn Abu Bakar claimed a phantom voting incident occurred at a polling centre in the constituency today. He said the alleged incident was detected at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Senggarang at 10.30am. Following the incident, Onn urged the Election Commission (EC) to ensure all eligible voters, particularly in the Senggarang constituency, are able to exercise their voting rights.

items.title: Typhoon Bavi: 900,000 evacuated in China, millions on alert across East Asia
items.summary: KEELUNG: More than 900,000 people have been evacuated in China as Typhoon Bavi brought heavy rain and strong winds to northern Taiwan and Japan's remote south-western islands, toppling trees and leaving tens of thousands without power. Extreme weather has already caused widespread disruption in southern and central China this week, with storms killing at least 39 people, causing rivers to overflow and triggering the collapse of a reservoir dam. Typhoon Bavi is expected to make landfall early on Sunday near Wenzhou, a city of nearly 10 million people in eastern Zhejiang province, where authorities said 887,801 people had been evacuated from their homes by late Friday.

items.title: Johor polls: Counting starts at 5pm, says EC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Election Commission (EC) said the counting of early votes for the Johor election will only begin at 5pm today. Early voting was held on July 7, while polling took place today. Earlier, graphics claiming that early vote counting was already underway circulated widely on social media.

items.title: Johor polls: Turnout crosses 63pc as more than 1.69 million voters cast ballots before polls close
items.summary: The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. At 3pm, the Semarang state constituency recorded the highest voter turnout at 66.1 per cent, followed by Sri Medan at 64.4 per cent and Penawar at 64.2 per cent. Penggaram recorded the lowest turnout at 48.8 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 49 per cent and Perling at 50.3 per cent.

items.title: Armed forces chief's Cambodia visit reinforces military ties
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Armed forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman's visit to Cambodia this week is expected to further strengthen military ties between the two countries. In a Facebook post today, Malek Razak said Malaysia and Cambodia were committed to elevating longstanding defence partnership through closer strategic cooperation and sustained high-level engagement. Earlier on July 9, he held bilateral talks with the Commander-in-Chief of the Royal Cambodian Armed Forces, General Vong Pisen.

items.title: Johor election: Voter turnout hits 63.13pct with two hours to go
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout for the Johor election reached 63.13 per cent as at 4pm today. The Election Commission, in its hourly update, said 1,693,625 of over 2.7 million eligible ordinary voters had cast their ballots. Early voting on Tuesday recorded a turnout of 95 per cent, with 19,471 voters casting their ballots.

items.title: Typhoon Bavi hits Taiwan, most areas suspend work and classes
items.summary: TAIPEI: Typhoon Bavi, the ninth typhoon of the year, reached north-eastern Taiwan early today, forcing flight cancellations in Taipei and Taichung and the suspension of work and classes across most parts of the island. Heavy rain was expected to continue throughout the day, with extremely heavy rainfall forecast in mountainous areas north of Taichung, while strong winds were reported in many areas. Meteorological officials said rainfall was expected to continue into the night in mountainous areas of central and southern Taiwan, before gradually easing on Sunday.

items.title: Former Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad quits Bersatu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad has resigned as Bersatu Tampin division chief and withdrawn his membership from the party, effective today. "I, Ridzuan Ahmad, hereby tender my resignation as Tampin Bersatu division chief, effective this Saturday, 11 July 2026," he said in the letter addressed to party secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali. [[nid:1486122]] Yesterday, Aziman Hadi Nazri said he had stepped down as a Bersatu Armada National executive council member and Supreme Council member.

items.title: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao link opens, easing border congestion
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM: A new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah with Thailand's Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex opened to the public today, aimed at easing congestion and improving cross-border movement. Motorist Mohd Faizal Ahmad, 42, from Penang, said the new route was better organised and provided greater convenience for Malaysians travelling to Thailand. We hope the new route will help reduce the problem," he said.

items.title: Busted in basement: Immigration smashes 'vault-like' Cheras vice den with steel doors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A syndicate went to extraordinary lengths to shield its suspected vice operation, turning an underground karaoke centre in Cheras into a "vault" fitted with multiple steel doors and escape routes to evade enforcement action. Kuala Lumpur Immigration director Hamsha Injau said initial investigations found that the premises charged up to RM1,300 for a six-hour session, which included the room, alcoholic beverages and GRO services. "Based on our initial investigations, we believe the foreign women were working voluntarily as GROs at the premises.

items.title: Shorts allowed at polling stations, EC clarifies after viral complaint
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Election Commission (EC) today clarified that voters are allowed to wear shorts at polling stations after a man claimed he and his family were denied entry to cast their ballots at a polling centre in Skudai. EC deputy chairman Dr Azmi Sharom said there was no official dress code prohibiting voters from wearing shorts or slippers when casting their votes. There is no such dress code," he said.

items.title: Typhoon Bavi forces Malaysia Airlines to cancel Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai flights
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — Malaysia Airlines has announced additional flight cancellations involving the Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai route due to adverse weather conditions associated with Typhoon Bavi, following yesterday’s advisory on cancellations involving the Kuala Lumpur-Taipei route. In a statement today, the national carrier said the affected flights on July 11 are MH386 (Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai), MH388 (Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai) and MH389 (Shanghai-Kuala Lumpur). Yesterday, Malaysia Airlines announced the cancellation of flights MH366 (Kuala Lumpur-Taipei) and MH367 (Taipei-Kuala Lumpur), originally scheduled to operate on July 11, due to Typhoon Bavi.

items.title: Opposition mayor among 36 detained in Turkey graft probe
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Turkish prosecutors have ordered the detention of 36 people, including the mayor of an Ankara district controlled by the main opposition party, as part of an investigation into alleged bribery and tender-rigging, the Ankara chief prosecutor's office said today. Authorities have detained 27 of the suspects, while searches continue for the remaining individuals, the prosecutor's office said in a statement. Huseyin Can Guner, mayor of Ankara's Cankaya district and a representative of the Republican People's Party (CHP), said in a post on X that he had informed authorities of his whereabouts and provided officers with a spare key to his home while he travelled to Ankara.

items.title: Kiandee asks Muhyiddin if Bersatu Supreme Council still has quorum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Suspended Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Ronald Kiandee has questioned whether the party's Supreme Council still meets the required quorum to hold valid meetings following the resignation, suspension and removal of several council members. Kiandee, who is Beluran member of parliament, said Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's assurance that the party's internal crisis would be resolved internally did not address the key issue of whether the Supreme Council still met the quorum requirement under Clause 13.12 of the party constitution to function and hold valid meetings. Aziman Hadi Nazri said he had stepped down as a Bersatu Armada National executive council member and Supreme Council member.

items.title: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao route opens to ease Malaysia-Thailand border congestion
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM, July 11 — The new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah with the Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex in Thailand was opened to the public today. One motorist, Mohd Faizal Ahmad, 42, from Penang, said the new route was better organised and offered greater convenience for Malaysians travelling to Thailand. We hope the new route will help reduce the problem,” he said.

items.title: Hundreds of thousands evacuated as Typhoon Bavi approaches China
items.summary: WENZHOU: China evacuated more than 600,000 people on Saturday ahead of Typhoon Bavi's expected landfall near the eastern city of Wenzhou, after the storm brought heavy rain and strong winds to Japan's southern Sakishima island chain and brushed past northern Taiwan. Although Bavi has slowed and weakened as it moves north-west over cooler waters, the typhoon remains a significant threat due to the large amount of moisture carried within its rain bands, which stretch across an area roughly the size of France. Bavi is forecast to make landfall near Wenzhou, a city home to about 10 million people, in the early hours of Sunday.

items.title: Turkiye fines over 100 doctors for Caesarian deliveries
items.summary: ANKARA: Turkiye's health ministry has fined more than 100 obstetrician-gynaecologists for carrying out Caesarian sections, suspending them from duty and forcing them to undergo training, BirGun newspaper reported on Saturday. The country has the highest rate of C-section births among the OECD's 38 nations, according to the last available data from 2023, with around 615 such procedures out of every 1,000 live births that year. "Turkiye's caesarean birth rate exceeds 60 per cent.

items.title: Fatal RXZ convoy crash: Motorcyclists were racing, say police
items.summary: KUANTAN: Several motorcyclists in a Yamaha RXZ convoy involved in a fatal crash along the East Coast Highway near Jabor were racing, police said. Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said preliminary investigations indicated this, adding that more than 30 motorcycles were involved in the crash. Ashari said police were examining dashcam footage showing the group performing dangerous stunts before the crash.

items.title: Abu Bakar to Shukri: Don't provoke political tension in Perlis
items.summary: KANGAR: Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah has advised former menteri besar Mohd Shukri Ramli against issuing statements that could trigger political tension in the state. He said the advice followed speculation that Shukri intends to table a motion of no confidence against his leadership at the upcoming Perlis state legislative assembly sitting. "The whole of Malaysia, and even the world, knows Tuanku's decree during the previous Perlis Dun (state) sitting.

items.title: UK police release man arrested on suspicion of killing former minister Widdecombe
items.summary: LONDON: A 26-year-old man arrested on suspicion of the murder of former British government minister Ann Widdecombe has been released and is no longer part of the investigation, police said on Saturday. "Our priority remains identifying those responsible and ensuring that all available evidence is thoroughly examined," Assistant Chief Constable Matt Longman said in a statement released by Devon and Cornwall police. Ambulance workers called police to Widdecombe's home in rural southwest England on Thursday, where she had been found dead after suffering serious injuries, police said.

items.title: Foreign worker killed in Kapar iron factory boiler fire
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A foreign factory worker was killed in a boiler fire at an iron processing plant in Kapar, Klang, this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters were alerted to the incident at 8.18am and immediately dispatched to the scene. "The operations commander reported that the fire involved a 20ft by 20ft boiler at the iron processing factory, but the blaze had already been extinguished by factory workers before firefighters arrived.

items.title: Isham Jalil applies to join Pas three years after Umno sacking
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno Supreme Council member Isham Jalil has applied to join Pas, three years after he was sacked from the party. In a Facebook post today, Isham said he submitted his membership application yesterday. "Taking the blessings of Friday, I completed and submitted my application to become a Pas member.

items.title: MOF expands STR cash payments to reach remote Orang Asli communities
items.summary: CAMERON HIGHLANDS: The Ministry of Finance (MOF) plans to conduct 10 Rahmah Cash Assistance (STR) payment programmes for Orang Asli communities in remote areas across Peninsular Malaysia to ensure recipients without bank accounts continue to receive government aid. Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican said the programme held in Cameron Highlands today was the sixth conducted this year under the initiative, which was introduced in 2024 to expand access to financial assistance and government services in remote communities. Johan told reporters this after visiting the programme at Dewan Datuk Mohd Nor @ Bah Ayob in Kampung Orang Asli Menson here today, where 113 Orang Asli recipients received STR payments totalling RM60,000.

items.title: Sekinchan padi field turns scene of rare funnel cloud sighting
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A funnel cloud was spotted over a padi field along Jalan Tali Air 4, Sekinchan near here this morning. Selangor Tourism and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Ng Suee Lim said preliminary information indicated that the phenomenon lasted between three and four minutes and occurred at about 11.40am. "The whirlwind formed in the middle of a padi field near the Sekinchan Terminal.

items.title: Kelantan fire dept to step up homestay, budget hotel fire safety checks
items.summary: TUMPAT: The Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department will intensify inspections of homestays, budget hotels and guesthouses across the state this year to ensure they comply with fire safety requirements. Its director Farhan Sufyan Borhan said the focus would be on homestays, as many were believed to be operating without registration, making fire safety monitoring more challenging. However, the department is taking proactive measures by warning owners to meet fire safety requirements before any incident occurs," he told reporters at the Fire Safety Campaign and Community Fire Point programme in Pulau Teluk Renjuna today.

items.title: Johor election: Five polling centres closed as of 2pm
items.summary: MERSING: Balai Raya Pulau Besar became the first polling centre to close in today's Johor election. Tenggaroh state constituency returning officer Jamil Hasni Abdullah said the centre, which had one polling stream and 32 registered voters, closed at 11am. "The polling centres at Balai Raya Pulau Aur and SK Pulau Pemanggil closed at noon, while Balai Raya Pulau Sibu and SK Pulau Tinggi closed at 2pm," he said.

items.title: Johari Ghani: Malaysia, Finland explore circular economy cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and Finland are exploring closer cooperation on circular economy implementation, with Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani saying the country's network of industrial parks could serve as a practical starting point for sustainable industrial development. Johari said this following a courtesy call from Finnish Ambassador to Malaysia Anne Vasara, during which both sides discussed opportunities to advance sustainable and competitive economic development through bilateral cooperation. "With approximately 600 industrial parks across the country, Malaysia is well positioned to adopt circular economy practices, beginning with our halal parks as a practical first step towards building a more sustainable and high-value industrial ecosystem," he said.

items.title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 56.77pc by 3pm with more than 1.52 million ballots cast
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.52 million voters, or 56.77 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 3pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. The Johor state election involves more than 2.6 million registered voters who are choosing representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.

items.title: Thunderstorm warning issued for eight states and KL as heavy rain, strong winds expected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to affect eight states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 5 pm today. The warning also covers Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim in Perak. Similar weather conditions are forecast in Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat in Selangor; Jelebu in Negeri Sembilan; and Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang and Mersing in Johor.

items.title: Pulau Besar becomes first Johor polling centre to close after 32 voters cast ballots
items.summary: MERSING, July 11 — Balai Raya Pulau Besar became the first polling centre to close in today’s Johor state election. “The polling centres at Balai Raya Pulau Aur and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Pulau Pemanggil closed at noon, while Balai Raya Pulau Sibu and SK Pulau Tinggi closed at 2 pm,” he told Bernama. All polling centres across the five islands cater to 690 registered voters.

items.title: Former Umno leader Isham Jalil applies to join PAS three years after party sacking
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — Former Umno Supreme Council member Isham Jalil has applied to join PAS, nearly three years after he was sacked from the party. Isham was sacked from Umno in December 2023, months after he was elected to the party’s Supreme Council during its internal elections. Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki had previously said Isham was removed for allegedly being disloyal, failing to follow party orders and directives, and not respecting party decisions.

items.title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 48.91pc by 2pm with more than 1.31 million ballots cast
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.31 million voters, or 48.91 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 2pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. The Johor state election involves more than 2.6 million registered voters who are choosing representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.

items.title: AirBorneo to open ticket sales for Kuching-Singapore flights ahead of July 22 launch
items.summary: KUCHING, July 11 — AirBorneo Airways will begin selling tickets for its inaugural international service between Kuching and Singapore on July 13, ahead of the launch of daily flights on July 22, which coincides with the Hari Sarawak celebration. “The new daily service between Kuching International Airport and Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4 will strengthen Sarawak’s connectivity with one of the region’s key aviation and business hubs, while opening new opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and Singapore,” the statement said. He said the Kuching-Singapore service marked a significant step in AirBorneo’s growth, extending Sarawak’s air connectivity beyond Malaysia’s borders for the first time.

items.title: Sarawak Immigration crackdown around Kuching, Kota Samarahan nets 24 foreign nationals
items.summary: KUCHING, July 11 — The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 24 foreign nationals during Op Minyak, Op Selera, and Op Mahir, which were carried out at seven locations around Kuching and Kota Samarahan yesterday. Inspections focused on heavy machinery workshops in Kampung Semeba and Kampung Sudat in Kuching, as well as eateries and premises suspected of conducting online gambling activities in Muara Tuang, Kota Samarahan. Several scrap metal and second-hand goods premises around Kota Samarahan were also inspected to ensure compliance with the country’s immigration laws.

items.title: LPT crash: More than 30 motorcycles believed involved in illegal racing, says police
items.summary: District police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said 18 victims were treated at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA), while two others received treatment at Sultan Ahmad Shah Medical Centre here. Earlier, Ashari said the crash, which occurred at about 1 am, claimed the lives of Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, who were all riding Yamaha RXZ motorcycles. He said another victim, Muhammad Safawi Md Noh, 24, died while receiving treatment at HTAA.

items.title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 42pc by 1pm, ahead of previous state election pace
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.13 million voters, or 42.16 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 1pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). In a Facebook post, the EC said the Semarang and Sedili state constituencies recorded the highest voter turnout at 50.5 per cent each, followed by Tanjung Surat at 48.6 per cent. Penggaram registered the lowest turnout at 32.2 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 32.6 per cent and Simpang Jeram at 35.2 per cent.

items.title: Johor polls: IGP says no candidates implicated in 90 election-related police reports
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Police received a total of 90 reports across Johor during the 14-day campaign period for the 16th Johor State Election, which ended at midnight yesterday. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said none of the reports involved election candidates or senior leaders of the contesting political parties. He also urged voters with complaints or grievances related to the election process to lodge reports with the police to enable the Election Commission and other relevant agencies to take prompt action.

items.title: Heavy rain and thunderstorms lash Johor, but voters brave the weather to keep polling on track
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Heavy rain and thunderstorms across Johor since midday today have not dampened the spirit of voters turning out for the 16th Johor state election. A Bernama check at several polling centres in Muar found continuous rain since morning affecting Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (SJK) Cina Limbong and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sri Muar. In Johor Bahru, similar weather conditions at SMK Bandar Uda Utama also failed to deter voters from playing their part, with polling centres remaining calm and orderly.

items.title: Smooth traffic at Johor Causeway, Second Link and main JB roads on polling day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 – Traffic conditions on several major routes in Johor were reported to be free-flowing to moderate on polling day despite an increase in the number of vehicles following the return of voters from Singapore. Checks at the main south-bound routes at the Skudai toll plaza and Kulai toll plaza showed a smooth traffic flow towards the Johor Bahru city centre. Despite the increase in vehicles entering the Johor Bahru city centre, traffic was reported to be at a moderate flow towards the afternoon.

items.title: Johor polls: More than 709,000 voters cast ballots by 11am as turnout hits 26.43pc
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 709,000 voters, or 26.43 per cent of registered electors, had cast their ballots as of 11am today in the 16th Johor state election, Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said. Ramlan said the turnout figure was recorded after polling began at 8am across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. The Johor state election sees more than 2.6 million registered voters casting their ballots to elect representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.

items.title: No ban on shorts at polling stations, says EC after voter allegedly stopped in Skudai
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — The Election Commission (EC) has clarified that there is no official dress code preventing voters from wearing shorts to polling stations, after an alleged incident involving a voter at a Skudai polling centre. “No such dress code. The images also showed the man speaking to a school security guard and a sign at the school gate listing a visitor dress code that prohibits male visitors from wearing shorts, ripped jeans, sleeveless tops and slippers.

items.title: Teo says MCMC received 29 complaints over online content during Johor campaign
items.summary: KULAI, July 11 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) received 29 complaints related to online content involving fake news, hate speech and scams during the campaign period for the 16th Johor state election. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said 17 of the complaints were related to the dissemination of fake news, while 11 involved hate speech and one concerned a fake account and impersonation. The 16th Johor state election sees 172 candidates contesting 56 State Legislative Assembly seats, with more than 2.6 million registered voters deciding the outcome today.

items.title: Penang governor hopes federal govt backs plan for Ceremonial Hall at Seri Mutiara
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 11 — Penang Yang Dipertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib has expressed hope that the federal government will give due consideration to a proposal to build a Ceremonial Hall within the Seri Mutiara grounds to enhance the conduct of official state ceremonies and uphold the dignity of the institution of the Head of State. He said such a facility is essential to ensure that investiture ceremonies for Penang’s state orders, decorations and medals are conducted with the appropriate decorum, tradition and grandeur. The governor said the proposed Ceremonial Hall would enable the state government to better fulfil its responsibility to uphold the dignity, honour and sovereignty of Penang.

items.title: Johor polls see 7.97pc turnout by 9am, surpassing previous election pace
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11— A total of 213,878 voters, or 7.97 per cent of the more than 2.6 million voters, had cast their ballots as of 9 am in the 16th Johor state election today. The Election Commission (EC), in a Facebook post, said the Mahkota state constituency recorded the highest voter turnout at 15.8 per cent, followed by Tanjung Surat (14.7 per cent) and Pasir Raja (13.6 per cent). Meanwhile, Bentayan and Simpang Jeram recorded the lowest voter turnout, with both registering 7.7 per cent, while Kukup recorded 7.6 per cent.

items.title: EC chairman makes rounds of Johor polling centres as voting continues smoothly
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun today inspected the voting process at four polling centres across the city to ensure the Johor state election was proceeding smoothly and in an orderly manner. He began his inspection at the Dewan Raya Taman Ungku Tun Aminah polling centre for the Skudai constituency, which has seven polling streams, at about 8.50 am. Ramlan then proceeded to the Raja Zarith Sofiah Library at Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) for the Senai constituency, which has two polling streams, followed by Sekolah Agama Taman Bukit Mewah in the Kempas constituency (six polling streams), before concluding his visit at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Bandar Uda Utama in the Perling constituency, which has nine polling streams.

items.title: Johor polls: Ministers, deputy ministers among early voters as state chooses new govt
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Several ministers and deputy ministers cast their ballots in the 16th Johor State Election this morning, exercising their rights as registered voters. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin exercised his voting rights at 8.30 am at Sekolah Kebangsaan Rinting 1, Masai, here. Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan cast his ballot at 8.40 am at Sekolah Kebangsaan Seri Bahagia, Benut.

items.title: Four Yamaha RXZ riders killed in 13-vehicle crash on East Coast Expressway
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 11 — Four motorcyclists were killed and several others injured in a 13-vehicle crash at KM246.4 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) near here early today. Kuantan police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said the crash, which occurred at about 1 am, claimed the lives of Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, who were all riding Yamaha RXZ motorcycles. Preliminary investigations found that the car, driven by a 25-year-old man from Kuantan, was travelling towards Kemaman when the crash occurred.

items.title: Onn Hafiz votes early in Simpang Renggam, urges Johoreans to turn out
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi cast his vote in the 16th Johor state election at Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Simpang Renggam at 8.33 am today. Before entering Stream 4 to cast his ballot, Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, took time to mingle with and greet other voters waiting for their turn. Seeking a second term, Onn Hafiz faces a straight fight against Nur Hafiz Roslan of Pakatan Harapan (PH).

items.title: Johor polls open with clear skies across most areas, but heavy rain dampens Muar
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Polling for the 16th Johor State Election began this morning under generally favourable weather conditions, with clear skies reported in most areas except for Muar, which experienced heavy rain from the early hours. A Bernama check at several polling centres found Election Commission (EC) officials, security personnel and media practitioners on site as early as 7am. Similar weather conditions were reported at Dewan Raya Taman Ungku Tun Aminah, Johor Bahru; Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Senggarang, Batu Pahat; and Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Cina (SJKC) Kulai.

items.title: MACC freezes 14 bank accounts, seizes RM1.4m assets in foreign affairs syndicate probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has frozen 14 bank accounts, comprising five company accounts and nine personal accounts belonging to several individuals, involving an estimated RM1.1 million, following investigations into a syndicate suspected of committing various offences related to foreign nationals affairs. “Yesterday, MACC also detained another enforcement officer at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya to assist in investigations. On Wednesday, through Op Layer (MACC Putrajaya), Op Dalang (MACC Selangor), Op Raider (MACC Negeri Sembilan) and Op Genuine (MACC Melaka), 38 individuals were arrested, with 33 remanded to assist in investigations.

items.title: Abang Johari says Sibu must move beyond timber industry with new economy
items.summary: SIBU, July 11 — Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg wants Sibu to be transformed into a low-carbon smart city driven by new technology, a green economy, and development based on a master plan to ensure the city remains competitive in the future. “Sibu must be developed according to its master plan. Abang Johari said that the development of Sibu will be driven through the Mid-Rajang Central Region Development Agency (MiRRDA) with the support of the Rajang Delta Development Agency (RADDA), which each received an allocation of RM1.5 billion to implement projects according to local needs through a bottom-up development approach.

items.title: Rafizi says Bersama aims to break Malaysia’s race-based politics with multiracial platform
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) sees the Johor state election as an opportunity for voters to move away from divisive race-based politics towards a more united platform for Malaysia’s future, said Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. The Bersama co-leader said he hoped the party and its candidates could win support from Malaysians of all races, reflecting the country’s diversity, in today’s polls. Also present was Bersama co-leader Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and the party’s 15 candidates contesting various seats across Johor.

items.title: Vigneswaran confident MIC can retain Indian support in Johor election
items.summary: KULAI, July 11 — MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran is confident that the Indian community will continue to support the Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in the Johor state election tomorrow, especially in the seats contested by MIC. He said that the confidence was based on the good relationship between the MIC and both the federal and state governments in addressing and resolving various issues involving the Indian community. The 16th Johor state election will see four candidates representing the MIC, namely K. Raven Kumar in the Kemelah state assembly seat; V. Rugendran (Kahang); P. Pannir Selvam (Perling); and R. Kumaran (Bukit Batu).

items.title: Anwar hits back at Sanusi over continued attacks despite Kedah development efforts
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, July 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed disappointment over Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor’s continued political attacks against him despite the Federal Government’s ongoing efforts to drive development in the state. When I went to Kedah, he did not even turn up, yet we brought the Thai Prime Minister there (to Bukit Kayu Hitam), not to Kuala Lumpur but to that remote area,” he said when speaking at the PH Grand Finale programme for the Layang-Layang state constituency here last night. He said bringing Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul to Bukit Kayu Hitam yesterday was aimed at officiating the opening of a new road alignment as part of efforts to boost the border economy, thereby benefiting the people of Perlis and Kedah.
